alerts.title: US-Iran reciprocal strikes continue; FM Araghchi demands full Iranian Hormuz control
alerts.summary: Despite tentative ceasefire, both US and Iran conducted additional strikes on 30 June. CENTCOM confirms strikes on multiple Iranian targets; Iran targeted commercial shipping near Strait of Hormuz. Iranian FM Araghchi has hardened negotiating demands in Baghdad talks, insisting full Iranian control over Hormuz and exclusion of foreign military presence. Nuclear breakout window remains 1-2 weeks. Sources: incidents (CENTCOM, PBS, NBC, BBC, AP News, CBS News, NYT), global_metrics (threat level 9/10 CRITICAL).
alerts.region: Persian Gulf, Strait of Hormuz, Iran

alerts.title: Iran-linked group claims hack of FBI drone systems; World Cup threat issued
alerts.summary: Iran-linked cyber group has publicly claimed successful infiltration of US FBI drone command systems and issued threat to target World Cup infrastructure. Represents escalation of Iranian cyber operations during active kinetic conflict phase. Comes amid elevated cyber threat landscape tied to Middle East war. Sources: global_metrics (cyber headlines), media reports (France 24, CBS News, CSIS).

alerts.title: Israel sustains multi-front operations across Gaza, Lebanon, Syria despite US-Iran pause
alerts.summary: IDF confirmed coordinated airstrikes on 30 June across three sectors: southern Syria (civilian evacuations), Gaza central zone (8+ killed including 2 children), and southern Lebanon border. Hezbollah publicly condemned new ceasefire arrangement. Coordinated multi-sector strikes indicate sustained Israeli operations independent of US-Iran negotiations. Sources: incidents (Al Jazeera, BBC, Reuters, NBC News, Religion News Service, Times of Israel).

alerts.title: US conditions fund unfreezing on nuclear progress despite agreed talks framework
alerts.summary: US stated it will not unfreeze any Iranian funds unless progress materializes on nuclear file, contradicting earlier agreement that nuclear matters would not be discussed at current talks stage. Creates asymmetric negotiating position and signals US intent to link non-nuclear economic relief to nuclear concessions. Heightens risk of talks collapse. Sources: telegram (ME Spectator ~48K views, 29 June), global_metrics (nuclear headlines).

alerts.title: US maintains forward naval presence: four carriers, three destroyers, submarines deployed
alerts.summary: US military asset positioning confirmed across theater: USS Eisenhower, Abraham Lincoln, Gerald R. Ford, Theodore Roosevelt (carriers); USS Carney, Mason, Laboon (destroyers); USS Florida (submarine in Persian Gulf); USS Bataan, Boxer (amphibious). Deployment maintains strategic pressure during negotiations while positioning for rapid escalation if talks collapse. Sources: assets (live-osint analysis of 54 articles).
alerts.region: Red Sea, Arabian Sea, Mediterranean, Pacific, Persian Gulf

analysis_note: US-Iran ceasefire remains fragile at day-two breakpoint with reciprocal strikes resuming; nuclear breakout window 1-2 weeks; Israel pursuing independent multi-front operations; cyber escalation and asymmetric negotiating tactics risk rapid collapse of diplomatic efforts.
